title: Client repository demo for local and S3 uploads
description: Add a runnable client demo/user guide that shows the Python client text UI uploading packages to a local repository and an S3-style repository, then confirms the completed packages in Catcher/web dashboard.
acceptance_criteria: Demo runs without real cloud credentials using a filesystem-backed S3 repository; client text UI shows local and S3 destination rows reaching completed/verified state; Catcher packages include local and S3 paths; client guide documents options and real S3/rclone substitution.

title: Client text UI for upload package tracking
description: Add a terminal/text display to the Python client so users can see files classified by package type and tracked through checksum/register/complete/failure states while ingest metadata is posted to Catcher.
acceptance_criteria: Client can classify common file types into package_type values, render a dependency-free text table of recent uploads, patch completed status/progress for registered packages, and README shows sample runs for user data, app logs, audit logs, business data, and cache-like files.

title: PC validation: end-to-end personal-computer backup scenario
description: Create an automated scenario that uses temporary local directories to emulate watch folders, NAS, Google Drive, and backup-service destinations. Validate initial send, log contents, interrupted/missing downstream copy, resend, and status output.
acceptance_criteria: The scenario runs in CI-like environments without cloud credentials; it fails if data is marked sent before checksum verification, if resend cannot restore a missing destination, or if status omits pending/failed work.

title: PC docs: personal backup quick start and recovery runbook
description: Write user-facing instructions for installing prerequisites, creating config, running the first backup, checking status, resending failed transfers, and doing a restore smoke test.
acceptance_criteria: A first-time user can follow the runbook on a personal computer; docs include safety notes for credentials, NAS mounts, Google Drive/rclone setup, and how to prove data can be recovered.

title: PC transport: local NAS/filesystem destination
description: Implement the first real destination adapter for local filesystem or mounted NAS paths, including directory creation, checksum verification, and permission/preflight checks.
acceptance_criteria: A personal computer can back up configured data to a local or mounted NAS path; missing permissions or unavailable mounts fail before data is marked sent; checksum verification is recorded.

title: PC CLI: init, run, status, resend, and restore commands
description: Provide a user-facing command interface for personal computers. Commands should initialize sample config, run a backup once, print status from local log/queue, resend failed or missing destinations, and run a restore smoke test.
acceptance_criteria: A user can run documented CLI commands without editing code; every command exits non-zero on unsafe config or failed verification; status explains what was sent and what remains pending.

title: PC client: reusable backup-chain engine from demo flow
description: Extract the send/verify/resend behavior proven in scripts/home-backup-chain-demo.py into reusable client code that is not tied to demo paths or fake sample data.
acceptance_criteria: The engine accepts configured files/destinations, packages data, verifies checksums, writes transfer-log records, and can be exercised by tests without real provider credentials.

title: PC client: watch folders and queue changed files safely
description: Replace the in-memory prototype watcher behavior with a durable queue suitable for personal computers. It must detect new/changed files, avoid partial-file reads, and persist pending work across restarts.
acceptance_criteria: A changed file in a configured watch folder becomes durable queued work; restarting the client does not lose pending work; partial/in-flight files are skipped until stable.

title: PC client: production transfer log and resend semantics
description: Promote the demo transfer-log concept into the client contract. Specify record fields, append-only behavior, failure records, idempotency keys, retention/rotation, and how resend selects a verified source copy.
acceptance_criteria: Transfer records are append-only JSONL; resend can reconstruct missing/corrupt downstream copies from logged metadata and verified local copies; corrupt logs fail loudly with actionable errors.

title: PC MVP: define installable backup client scope and default flow
description: Turn the current prototype and local provider-chain demo into an explicit personal-computer MVP contract: supported operating systems, default watch folders, destination types, required commands, local state files, and what is intentionally out of scope for first users.
acceptance_criteria: OpenSpec and README describe the first-user flow end to end; success criteria include configure, run, verify, status, resend, and restore-smoke-test steps; unsupported behavior is named explicitly.

title: PC client: configuration file schema and validation
description: Define and implement a local config file for source id, watch paths, staging path, transfer log path, local NAS destination, cloud/offsite destinations, schedule, and Catcher URL. Validate paths and destination declarations before any backup starts.
acceptance_criteria: A sample config can be validated from the CLI; invalid watch paths or destination definitions fail before transfer; docs include a minimal personal-computer config.

title: Personal computer backup MVP
description: Make Edge Backup usable by people on personal computers. Scope is an installable local client that watches configured folders, packages and verifies data, sends it to a local NAS and at least one offsite/cloud destination, records a durable local transfer log, supports resend/restore validation, and reports status to Catcher/Text UI when available.
acceptance_criteria: A non-developer can follow documented steps on a personal computer to configure watch folders and destinations, run a backup, verify copies, inspect what was sent, rerun/resend after an interrupted transfer, and confirm status locally or through Catcher.

title: P1: Text UI — one-shot status report (component status, buckets)
description: Python + Rich script; component status, buckets; CATCHER_URL; scripts/text-ui.py

title: P1: Text UI — packages, clients, rules, projections
description: DataGrid-style tables for packages, clients, retention rules, projections; mirrors web dashboard content

title: P1: Text UI — live refresh mode (--live, CATCHER_URL)
description: --live flag; periodic refresh (default 3s); TUI or watch-style redraw

title: P1: Compact dashboard — remove Data Flow, component status as nav links
P1: Retention rulesets — view + PATCH update (MVP)
P1: Package progress bar, checksum when computed
P1: Deleted data — delete oldest keep latest messaging
P1: Rename jobs→packages, scrollable filterable list, dropdown details
P1: Dark mode dashboard (IxDF aligned)
description: Dark theme, IxDF signifiers

title: P1: Playwright snapshots updated for dark mode
P1: Fix Svelte $state rune (.svelte.js stores)
P1: Mock data for transfer validation (MANIFEST.json, fixtures)
P1: Dashboard IxDF signifiers and feedback
P1: Docker Compose (catcher + client + dashboard)
P1: Dashboard sources list view
P1: Client script in container (watch, POST /ingest)
P1: Playwright validation (health, dashboard, sources)
P1: Init catcher backend (FastAPI)
P1: Checksum required when size_bytes > 0
P1: Define API routes (ingest, jobs, sources)
P1: Init frontend (Svelte) and dashboard
PC transport: rclone destinations for Google Drive and backup services
description: Add rclone-backed destination support for Google Drive and generic remotes used as backup services. Keep credentials in rclone config; the client references remote names and paths only.
acceptance_criteria: Given an existing rclone remote, the client can send and verify a package to Google Drive or another configured remote; missing rclone/remote configuration produces a clear preflight failure.

title: PC transport: restic repository backup and restore smoke test
description: Integrate restic for full backup repositories where dedupe/integrity are desired. Include repository preflight, backup execution, snapshot verification, and a small restore smoke test.
acceptance_criteria: A configured restic repository can receive a backup; the client records snapshot metadata; a restore smoke test proves at least one file can be restored and checksum-matched.

title: P2: DataGrid short-term improvements (package_type richselect, filter config, BucketCell)
description: Per OpenSpec §1.4: richselect for package_type column; filter config (search icon, clear) on path/source_id; use BucketCell for bucket column

title: P1: Dashboard UI refinements (Toaster, full-width, Clients tile)
description: Toaster for feedback; full-width layout; Sources→Clients; remove reload button; Clients before Packages

title: P1: Package grid bucket column with bucket colors
description: BucketCell component; bucket column uses hot/warm/cold/offsite badge colors

title: P1: Buckets with file count, storage labels, incoming by time
description: Bucket cards spaced, explicit Files/Storage labels, incoming_1h metric for packages arrived in window

title: P1: Clients DataGrid (DNS, IP, type, version, in-progress, last upload)
description: Clients panel as DataGrid with dns_name, ip, client_type, client_version, in_progress_count, last_package_uploaded

title: P1: Package types and per-type retention rule sets
description: user_data, app_logs, audit_logs, business_data, job_package, cache; rule sets per type; replicate_to_all for business data; DataGrid of rules

title: P2: Package Windows tooling for deployment
description: Package the Windows personal-computer client for repeatable installation, startup configuration, and updates.
acceptance_criteria: Installer or packaged artifact sets up prerequisites/config locations, preserves local transfer logs on upgrade, and documents uninstall/recovery behavior.

title: P2: Windows endpoint agent (monitor local folders)
description: Build the Windows endpoint version of the personal-computer backup client after the shared PC client CLI/config/log foundation exists. It should monitor local folders, run backups with the configured destinations, and report status clearly to a Windows user.
acceptance_criteria: Windows user can install/run the client against the personal-computer MVP config, back up a test folder, verify destination copies, inspect local status, and trigger resend/restore smoke-test commands.

title: P4: Cloud storage tiers (hot/warm/cold) and offsite
description: Implement production cloud/offsite storage tiers using rclone/restic destinations once the personal-computer client abstractions and validation are in place.
acceptance_criteria: Configured cloud/offsite destinations receive verified packages or restic snapshots, lifecycle behavior is documented, and failures are visible locally and through Catcher where configured.

title: P3: Linux/macOS clients and network filesystems
description: Extend the personal-computer client to Linux/macOS and optional network filesystem sources after the Windows/client foundation is proven.
acceptance_criteria: Linux/macOS users can run equivalent config, backup, status, resend, and restore-smoke-test flows using platform-appropriate scheduling.
